Pre-Ordered this game and started playing on launch day. Loved it. There were problems, but I figured that they'd get weeded out eventually. Got bored and stopped playing for a few months. Came back, same problems. I figured that they were busy working on updates, which they were to a certain extent. They were busy re-working the intro to the game two separate times. This game had immense potential for being one of the first VRMMO's created. Now it lies discontinued and forgotten by most, with a dwindling player base that feels betrayed.
It actually breaks my heart to see how far they had come only to be ruined by Nexus. RIP Zenith. There could have been so much more of it...

So I hear about and pre ordered this game before it even released back when I had a quest 1, and I've been watching it change since then and have stopped by to see what's new. This game was really my hope for a mmorpg vr game, and they were doing well until they added the new game mode while also changing the story line and then just ceasing any updates from there. If the devs ever decide to update this again, please fix it back to the way it was. I still love this game and even though its dead I still come back to see how its doing, really hope it doesnt just die out. Amazing game with a lack of players due to a final poor update.

Best regards 
Another guy

reviews.review_title: offline mode or lan network please
reviews.review_description: I've bought the game three times to play with my wife and son. Please, if you're going to shut down the servers, leave a LAN mode so the three of us can play together. We love Sword Art Online, and this game is very similar. 🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏

I love playing Zenith The Last City! The awesome adventures in virtual reality never end! Zenith The Last City truly is an amazing immersive game to play. Zenith is a multi-player game, guilds can be formed so people can have fun playing together as a team.  There's so much to do and discover in Zenith! You can fly, soar like an eagle, go zip lining, swing at great heights using a grappling tool, run like the wind, jump from great heights, the exciting fun is endless! I highly recommend this amazing game!

I rarely write negative reviews, but Zenith VR left me genuinely frustrated. From the very beginning, the experience collapses under poor design choices. Being forced to create an external account before even entering the game is already problematic, but doing so in VR—with clumsy interfaces and awkward typing—is simply unacceptable. It’s evident this title was developed with flat-screen gameplay in mind and awkwardly ported to VR, with almost no real consideration for immersive mechanics.
The game is riddled with bugs and crashes, breaking any sense of continuity or enjoyment. Nothing feels intuitive: menus are poorly placed, quest objectives hover awkwardly in the corner of your vision, and you constantly have to strain your eyes just to read basic information. Movement is stiff and uncomfortable, the grappling system is poorly executed, and overall, there is nearly zero interactivity that makes VR feel alive.
Graphically, it’s uninspiring and dated. There is no immersion whatsoever—it feels more like fighting with the controls than actually playing a game. What’s marketed as an MMORPG turns out to be a broken, unfinished mess that offers no excitement, no discovery, and certainly no fun.
I’ve tried many VR titles, but this stands out as one of the worst experiences I’ve ever had. It’s astonishing that a game released in this state is presented as a full VR MMORPG. Absolutely nothing works as it should. Frustrating, uninspired, and deeply disappointing—Zenith VR is, quite frankly, a complete failure.

reviews.review_title: Still one of the best VR games ever
reviews.review_description: The game is definitely worth saving! Please buy the DLC which is the core game which has dungeons, raids, flying, Guilds and world bosses. It will start you off in Skyland but I suggest doing the old world content first by going through the portal on the ship after level 5 to go to the zones. Once out of the go left across the bridge to talk to rasken to start the original story quests and find the Amara statue. Please share this game with friends to get this game popular again so Ramen will revisit this game through love & support that has so much potential. If you are a new player we still have a few thousand players that raid daily and play together. My guild players mostly on aether Shard or seek me out on discord Derrick2357 to get added. The only rules namewe have it to be respectful because we have members across all spectrums that will always be helpful and courteous  but we definitely live up to our guilds name which is Insanity.

I will be mostly reviewing the free part, Zenith: Infinite Realms 

The core loop is simple: jump through floating island maps, kill mobs, and repeat until you hit the boss. Combat's fun when it works—you’ve got a magic spell, throwable blades, and a bow for long range. Once you start unlocking legendary gear (around level 25), things get way more fun. At level 83 (I think) you can get “Unique” gear that makes your spells feel like you're an anime god. 

The game is absolutely janky. Sometimes a run breaks and you have to reset. The maps are reused constantly, and the community is mostly quiet or new players. But there’s a leaderboard system, and chasing the top spot is surprisingly addicting. It makes the grind feel worth it. 

Jank isn't always a bad thing. Because of the jankiness of spells, they can concentrate and do explosive damage to bosses if they walk into them just right. This influences the way you fight and honestly it feels like a unique twist. 

There’s no more content coming, which is a bummer. But for a free game, if you like competitive leaderboards spots, spell-flinging, and quick session-based VR fun, it’s absolutely worth trying. Just keep your expectations in check.

The DLC is $10 and unlocks the MMO part of the game. I do think it's fun, however there are so little players left. And for dungeons, you have to have multiple people to even start them. You may be able to find a couple of players in the discord willing to run dungeons with you, but it isn't a guarantee. 

Fast flying is very fun and when you get to the Celestial Throne, the track slaps. However, the "Not enough memory to run Zenith" bug seems to be happening more than ever. It was a bit frustrating before, and it's worse than it was.

I just started playing the game and i realy enjoyed it. Literally started it today. And i already play it around 6+ hours non stop. One thing that kept me asking is why the server is so deserted? It is so sad. I hope this games still going or by miracle the game got resurrected by the dev I'll be really happy. I would not mind even if i have to pay for another DLC it realy is a tealy great game and very comfortable. I never had a single motion sickness playing this game.

reviews.review_title: This game used to be good
reviews.review_description: When this game used to just be said the last city, and when it first came out, I remember me and my friends are so excited, battling dungeons together all being in different classes, and just exploring the world and the story itself, but the moment Infinite realms came out it just became another VR chat and rec room. Don’t get me wrong. They’re OK games but they don’t have the feeling of the reason I got virtual reality. I wanted to be in a whole other world like sword art online and that was the one game that was so close to becoming it but they just started focusing more on infinite realms and forgot all about the last city ￼
The best game I’ve ever played ￼
I really enjoy the game i have over 140gb of free space but the device keeps saying low memory regardless

When it first came out, it was exactly what I dreamed VR to be. The first months after release remain the peak of my VR experience to date. 
The first updates were nice QoL but things eventually got lost somehow. The F2P gamble offered a service that no one wanted and shifted focus away from was special. I think it hurt the game overall, cheapened it.

Mainly due to player counts dwindling, I regretfully moved onto other things, I often wonder if I had IRL friends with VR, there could be a use case to keep playing Zenith... my last days of playing were spent relaxing around scenic areas in the hopes of finding another player, listening to music and watching videos with a PC overlay, a new player came along, I taught them how to activate the REAL flight controls (that should be default), they were happy, i wonder how long they kept playing for.

I would re-release it if I really knew my stuff, remove F2P elements but drop the price, unite the player base in that sense. Itd be cool if 20 active players on a server was enough to feel populated. I can think of so many ways to do this! A simple 'warp to next nearest party/player' would be an example.

The game's got good bones - there must be a way to save it.

reviews.review_title: How many reviews did Ramen pay for?
reviews.review_description:
I can't believe all these reviews are real people.

The first thing you see when you've finished character creation is a premium cosmetic store. You don't know what your character is wearing at this point. 

The second thing you see is a black tear through VR reality as the game engine tries to catch up with the minecraft graphics. I've never lagged in VR before - I wanted to puke. 

After managing through that, and then playing through the weird "tutorial" that is given to you from a random person you find after roaming, you realize you don't know anything about the game. Or the world, or its lore. You know nothing, but you go into a dungeon anyway, because gaming. 

The initial combat is subpar to every other game you've played. it's nothing new or impressive, it's not rewarding or responsive. 

You're disappointed, so you Google Zenith. You see a reddit post about how the game went through several rounds of donation drives and kickstarters before having a big company donate 35 million dollars into it several years ago. 

You then realize that the devs don't know how to make a game. They played around with an idea, garnered hype and money for it, and then were either too talentless or paying themselves too much to make it work. That's why you saw a premium store before you saw the game. 

You uninstall. You won't get your time back.
paid for the game when it was good and then they moved their focus to their bad f2p mode and abandoned the old game. the devs laugh at the people that paid too

reviews.review_title: No respect for the players time
reviews.review_description:
It's absolutely ridiculous this is the final product. So you boot up the game and wait for it to load. Then you create an account.

Then another loading screen.

Then you pick your name.

Then another loading screen.

Then you create your character.

Then another loading screen.

Then the game crashes.

Seriously, you need a loading screen for a virtual keyboard to type your name? I find it hard to believe this went through any sort of testing and someone thought that many loading screens was a good idea. I wish I could tell you about the actual game but I tried 3 times in a row for over 15 minutes trying to get into the game and it kept crashing. So save yourself the trouble and play something else that has more respect for your time.

UPDATE 2/29/2024:
Devs are now banning anybody that speaks negatively about the game, both from their Discord and in-game. They are also removing posts from their subreddit that say anything remotely resembling criticism. Stay away from this developer, can’t accept any criticism and lashes out when they receive it.

reviews.review_title: VERY POOR GRAPHICS. NO STORY. Waste of $
reviews.review_description:
I tried to return this purchase silently but they denied my refund so here’s my 30 bucks.
The game seemed to have great reviews on YouTube and other platforms. However upon downloading it for myself and playing it. I discovered quickly that the graphics are absolutely terrible and the game play is less than compelling because of this. Also there was a story to the game. It seems like a full on beta PS1 game. So don’t be bought out by these descriptions of the game having beautiful landscapes to gaze upon lol. That’s not a thing. My advice, take your 30 bucks and literally buy any other game. You’ll definitely be happier. Especially considering how stringent the return policy is. They took my money and all I’m left with is a game barely worth 4.99.

developer_response.body: Hello Lokinesss, We are working to improve certain aspects of the game that you had mentioned as well as the first time user experience. Hopefully in the future you decide to give the game another try.
